title: Visakhapatnam horror: Navy staffer kills woman, dismembers body, stores remains in fridge
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Vizag as a Naval technician allegedly murdered his girlfriend, dismembered her body, and disposed of some parts. The accused surrendered to police after confessing to the brutal crime. Investigators recovered some body parts from his refrigerator, but the head remains missing, prompting a dedicated search.

title: 'Dubai could be blown up': US economic expert flags risks for UAE if it enters Iran war
content: Top US economist Jeffrey Sachs warned the UAE that its alignment with the US and Israel could expose Dubai and Abu Dhabi to serious threats if it enters the ongoing Iran war. He criticized the Abraham Accords as an "invitation for disaster," arguing that overreliance on American security guarantees is a dangerous miscalculation for the Gulf nation.

title: Water discovery fuels return to Moon
content: Four astronauts are set to launch on a historic lunar mission, marking humanity's return to the Moon after over 50 years. This revival of crewed lunar exploration was significantly fueled by the discovery of water by India's Chandrayaan-1, which reignited global interest and provided crucial scientific justification for future missions.

title: 'KKR fully aware': Cricket Australia forced to clarify on Green after Rahane's 'ask CA' jibe
content: Cricket Australia clarified Cameron Green's inability to bowl for Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026, citing a lower back injury. This came after KKR skipper Ajinkya Rahane's pointed remarks about the Australian board. Green is undergoing managed rehabilitation and is expected to resume bowling in 10-12 days, with KKR fully informed.

title: Dhurandhar: The Revenge box office collection day 12: Film eyes Rs 1000 crore
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has officially made history with its day 11 collections. The film enters its second Monday and day 12, today,  March 30, 2026. In a stunning display of box office dominance, the Ranveer Singh starrer has surpassed the lifetime India net of its predecessor, 'Dhurandhar Part 1' with its Rs. 840.20 crore, in just 11 days - a feat that originally took 15 weeks. With a current domestic net of Rs. 848.55 crore and a global gross of Rs. 1,361.95 crore, the Aditya Dhar directorial is now chasing the elite Rs. 1,000 crore India net club.

title: 'Umpires ne bola side ho jaayein': First-hand account of PSL ball-tampering controversy
content: Pakistan Super League (PSL) is embroiled in a ball-tampering scandal after a controversial incident involving Lahore Qalandars players. Umpires awarded five penalty runs to Karachi Kings after deeming the ball deliberately altered, leading to a reduced target and a Lahore Qalandars loss. Opener Fakhar Zaman has denied charges of ball-tampering.

title: Petrol, diesel price: Global crude oil costs rise; what's the situation in India?
content: Last week, the government announced a big excise duty cut on petrol and diesel prices, in effect cushioning consumers from a hike, while also reducing the blow for oil marketing companies.

title: Google founder to employees at town hall: This year a big role will be played by AI agents
content: Google employees are buzzing about Agent Smith, an internal AI tool automating tasks and accessible via mobile. Its popularity led to restricted access, highlighting Google's focus on AI agents for productivity in 2026, as emphasized by cofounder Sergey Brin. AI adoption is becoming mandatory across the company, with usage even impacting performance reviews.

title: ‘Rohit 2.0 will worry all IPL teams’: Kumble hails MI star’s blazing return
content: Anil Kumble lauded Rohit Sharma's explosive 78 off 38 balls, heralding a formidable 'Rohit 2.0' ready to dominate the IPL. Kumble highlighted Rohit's effortless mastery over quality bowlers, suggesting this revitalised form will pose a significant threat to all opposing teams. He also critiqued KKR's spin strategy, particularly their delayed use of Sunil Narine, which proved costly.

title: Operations normalise in many factories as commercial LPG supplies improve
content: Liquefied petroleum gas is extensively used across industries such as automobiles and electronics, particularly in processes like brazing and paint shop operations, as well as in segments like food processing.

title: Research gives 15 reasons to tell everyone that why using ChatGPT as therapist can be dangerous
content: AI chatbots like ChatGPT pose significant risks for mental health support, a Brown University study revealed. Researchers identified 15 key dangers, including poor crisis management, deceptive empathy, and potential discrimination. Unlike human therapists, AI lacks professional oversight and accountability, raising concerns about patient safety and ethical standards in emotional guidance.

title: From IIT failure to Forbes list: Rise of Alakh Pandey
content: Alakh Pandey, a teacher from Prayagraj, transformed his YouTube channel into the edtech giant PhysicsWallah, making quality education accessible and affordable for millions. His journey from free online lessons to a billion-dollar valuation highlights a unique path to success in the competitive Indian education market.

title: Watch: Video reveals mid-air collision between American Airlines jet & Black Hawk helicopter
content: New footage reveals the exact moment an American Airlines jet collided with a US Army Black Hawk helicopter near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport. The tragic January 29th crash resulted in 67 fatalities. In response, the FAA implemented safety changes, including restricting helicopter traffic and overhauling the air traffic control system.

title: PSL hotel security breach! 'Guests' till 1:25 am; player says 'Afridi not to be blamed'
content: Lahore Qalandars captain Shaheen Afridi and player Sikandar Raza are accused of breaching hotel security protocols by hosting four guests in Raza's room until 1:25 a.m., despite repeated denials from security. Raza claims it was his request to meet family and friends, stating he and Afridi were unaware of updated SOPs.

title: In Census, 'stable' live-in couple to be counted as married
content: Census 2027 will recognize live-in couples in stable unions as married.  The self-enumeration portal clarifies that such relationships should be treated as married. This marks a public acknowledgment, though officials note similar declarations were accepted in past censuses.  The question is part of the houselisting operations.

title: Pakistan a base for major terror groups, some active since 1980s: US Congress report
content: A US Congressional report highlights Pakistan as a base for numerous armed and terrorist organizations, some active since the 1980s. Despite military operations, many designated groups continue to operate from its soil, reinforcing concerns about Pakistan's links to terrorism. This has led to recent escalations and subsequent de-escalation efforts between India and Pakistan.

title: Bimal Oberoi says 'Dhurandhar' character Shirani can have a spin-off; praises Aditya Dhar
content: Bimal Oberoi envisions a vibrant future for the 'Dhurandhar' universe that extends well beyond his role as Shirani. He proposes delving into the intriguing backgrounds of characters like Rakesh Bedi, Danish Pandor, Akshaye Khanna’s Rehman Dakait, and Mustafa Ahmed’s Rizwan, emphasizing the ensemble’s multifaceted nature. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: Where next for US troops? Iran's 400kg uranium in focus as Trump weighs endgame
content: The US is expanding its military presence in the Middle East with hundreds of additional troops, including special forces, to bolster options amid escalating tensions with Iran. These forces are being positioned to potentially secure the Strait of Hormuz, seize Kharg Island, or target nuclear sites, reflecting a broader posture shift as the conflict evolves.

title: Delhi classes 9, 11 result to be released soon: Check details here
content: The Directorate of Education (DoE), Delhi, is expected to release Class 9 and 11 results for the 2025–26 session in April 2026 on edudel.nic.in. Students can check scores using login details under the Results section. They must verify marksheets carefully and report errors, while regularly tracking the official website for updates and announcements.

title: Delhi Police nab Lashkar-e-Taiba commander Shabbir Ahmed Lone near Bangladesh border
content: Delhi Police's Special Cell has apprehended Lashkar-e-Taiba commander Shabbir Ahmed Lone near the India-Bangladesh border. Lone, a key operative, was allegedly operating a LeT module from a hideout near Dhaka. The arrest followed extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering on cross-border terror activities.

title: No rust despite rest as Kohli does Kohli things
content: Virat Kohli showcased imperious form upon his return to T20 cricket, scoring an unbeaten 69 off 38 balls to guide Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket win. He attributed his smooth transition to recent ODI success and dedicated fitness work, emphasizing the importance of breaks for staying fresh and motivated.

title: Gold price prediction: Is the yellow metal in bearish territory? Key levels to watch out
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices seem to be exhibiting a corrective phase with a bearish tone, feels Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd. The analyst shares his outlook on the yellow metal:

title: Rupee rebounds from record low: Currency rises 128 paise to 93.57 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee saw a significant rebound, gaining 128 paise to 93.57 against the US dollar following RBI's directive to limit banks' currency exposure. This move prompts banks to reduce dollar holdings, offering temporary support.

title: Record broken! Rohit Sharma dethrones Virat Kohli
content: Rohit Sharma's explosive 78 off 38 balls, coupled with Ryan Rickelton's 81, powered Mumbai Indians to a six-wicket victory over KKR in their IPL 2026 opener. The duo's record 148-run stand secured MI's highest successful home chase. Rohit also set a new IPL record for most runs against a single opponent.

title: Target hit by Iran: Images show destroyed US E-3 Sentry at Saudi Arabia's Prince Sultan base
content: Iran claims to have destroyed a US E-3 Sentry aircraft during a missile and drone strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. Images show a burned-out E-3 Sentry, a critical airborne surveillance and battle management platform. This incident occurs amidst escalating tensions and retaliatory attacks between Iran, the US, and Israel.

title: Rameez questions PSL ball-tampering on air; Afridi says 'we'll check camera'
content: Lahore Qalandars captain Shaheen Afridi addressed a controversial final over where teammate Fakhar Zaman was accused of ball tampering. The incident led to five penalty runs for the opposition, Karachi Kings, significantly impacting the match's outcome. Afridi stated the team would review the footage to understand the situation fully.

title: Centre rolls out PDS kerosene in 60-day relief measure for consumers amid LPG strain
content: Facing global energy disruptions, the Centre has launched a 60-day emergency plan, distributing extra kerosene to states for cooking and lighting. This move aims to support families experiencing LPG shortages, with authorities also intensifying raids against hoarding and black marketing to ensure supply chain integrity.  Meanwhile, some consumers are switching to piped natural gas.

title: You may be breathing wrong all day: Doctors explain how it affects your brain& heart
content: Did you know that many of us are caught in a cycle of shallow breathing? This pattern doesn’t just leave us feeling anxious—it can also affect our cognitive abilities and heart health. Dr. Sushil Gupta highlights that shallow breaths can activate our fight-or-flight response, making it hard to concentrate and even causing irregular heart rhythms.

title: How a village potter invented a refrigerator that works without electricity
content: A Gujarati potter, Mansukhbhai Prajapati, revolutionized rural India with his electricity-free clay refrigerator. Born from the aftermath of the 2001 earthquake, this innovative device uses evaporation to keep food and water cool for days. Priced affordably, it offers a vital solution for households facing power cuts and limited resources, proving that practical innovation can emerge from traditional skills.

title: Saudi, Kuwait, Bahrain 'fighting back': Trump claims Gulf nations '100% on US side'
content: President Trump declared Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain are fully aligned with the US against Iran, stating they are "100% on our side." He noted these Gulf nations were surprised by Iran's missile and drone attacks but are now fighting back effectively. The ongoing conflict, now in its fourth week, has seen escalating exchanges and regional instability.

title: IndiGo crew averts disaster as UP man tries to open door mid-air, claims he is ‘possessed’
content: A passenger claiming to be 'possessed' attempted to open an emergency exit door on a Bengaluru-Varanasi IndiGo flight. Crew members swiftly intervened, preventing the man from opening the door on two separate occasions. The pilot temporarily ascended as a precaution before the flight landed safely, and the passenger was detained.

title: Indian worker killed in Iranian attack on Kuwait power, desalination plants
content: “A service building at a power and water desalination plant was attacked as part of the Iranian aggression against the State of Kuwait, resulting in the death of an Indian worker and significant material damage to the building,” ministry spokesperson Fatima Abbas Jawhar Hayat said. Amid rising tensions, Pakistan said it was willing to facilitate and host “meaningful talks” between Washington and Tehran in an effort to end the month-long US-Israel war with Iran.

content: UConn eliminated No. 1 overall seed Duke from the men's NCAA tournament on Braylon Mullins' 3-pointer with 0.4 seconds left.

content: Eagles general manager Howie Roseman had a stock answer for every question about receiver A.J. Brown that he fielded from the local media at the league meetings Sunday, opting for a neutral response amid the ongoing trade speculation.

content: The Cleveland Browns will not be signing AJ Epenesa, with the team uncomfortable finalizing the deal after the veteran defensive end had his physical, a source told ESPN on Sunday.

content: NFL owners are "alarmed" by the state of negotiations with the NFL Referees Association and have authorized staff members to begin hiring and onboarding replacement officials in the coming weeks, league sources said Sunday.

content: Gary Woodland won the Houston Open by five shots Sunday, his first PGA Tour victory since undergoing brain surgery in 2023.

content: Bo Bichette heard his first boos from Mets fans as he has struck out eight times in his first three games. "I thought my at-bats were terrible, too," he said.

content: Yes, the timing of a safety car helped Kimi Antonelli to Sunday's win at the Japanese GP, but he was still the fastest car on track when it happened.

title: Revisiting Dasgaon, where a 23-year-old drank water from a well and broke a social order
content: Months before the Mahad Satyagraha of 1927, a similar symbolic act by the people of Dasgaon in Maharashtra took place. A group of about 300, led by Ramchandra Babaji More, drew water from a well and a lake and drank it. The satyagrahis were Dalits, implementing a resolution to open public water bodies to them. Now, 100 years later, the people of Dasgaon have forgotten the event

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Still waiting for your Voter ID? Download it online

title: What's next for Duke and Notre Dame after their Elite Eight exit?
content: all four no. 1 seeds advanced to the elite eight of the 2026 women's tournament . the offseason officially starts for some of them . here's your guide to their expected departures, returnees and top incoming recruits .

title: What's next for Duke, Tennessee after Elite Eight: Recruits, transfer portal, more
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament has featured its fair share of heartbreaking losses . storied programs didn't reach the regionals such as Kansas and North Carolina . ESPN's Jeff Borzello and Myron Medcalf outline expected departures and returnees .

title: Power Rankings: Each franchise's career playoff points leader
content: teams are battling for positioning and playoff spots . some players could add to their playoff point totals . a panel of hockey commentators, analysts, reporters and editors send in a 1-32 poll based on the games through Wednesday .

title: Sunday's Elite Eight analysis: Inside UConn's stunning win over Duke
content: freshman Braylon Mullins sinks a logo 3-pointer with less than a second to go . the Huskies advance to the final Four for the third time in four years . a flurry of 3-pointers in the final four minutes pushed them over the top .

title: Early Men's Final Four preview: Arizona-Michigan, UConn-Illinois predictions
content: the 2026 men's final four is set for saturday's national semifinals against Illinois . the final weekend of the tournament is the final of the NCAA tournament . for the season, Arizona ranked fifth in the country in paint points, averaging 42 .

title: NHL playoff watch: Updated East, West wild-card-race projections
content: in the east, the Boston Bruins and Columbus Blue Jackets hold the precious wild-card positions . they are trailed most closely by the Ottawa Senators and Detroit Red Wings . the Philadelphia Flyers are also in action Sunday (7 p.m. ET, ESPN+ ).

title: Why NBA insiders are all-in on the Spurs: 'Victor breaks all the rules'
content: the Oklahoma City Thunder ended their longest road trip of the season with a loss to the Boston Celtics . it was the Thunder's first loss in a month . the San Antonio Spurs are a remarkable 23-2 since Feb. 1 .
